title: Preserve or document array cardinality compatibility
description: Recent generator changes allow array responses without minItems to be empty, where previous generated tests rejected empty arrays by default. Existing consumers that relied on COT rejecting empty arrays may now pass silently. Preserve the old non-empty default unless an OpenAPI-strict cardinality mode is explicitly selected, or treat the change as breaking with clear version/changelog/readme coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Array responses without minItems either retain the prior non-empty default by default, or the behavior change is explicitly documented as breaking with release-note coverage and tests confirming the chosen compatibility policy.

title: Scope generated property specs to avoid name collisions
description: Generated property specs are registered globally by bare property name, including inline response properties. Schemas or responses that reuse the same property name with different shapes can overwrite each other and make generated tests validate against the wrong contract. Generate owner/path-scoped spec keywords, or otherwise avoid global bare-name s/def collisions while preserving unqualified payload keys.
acceptance_criteria: Two schemas or inline responses may reuse a property name with incompatible shapes without overwriting each other's generated specs; req-un/opt-un payload keys remain unqualified; regression tests cover component and inline collision cases.

title: Fix enum specs for false and nil values
description: Generated specs currently emit OpenAPI enum values as a raw Clojure set. Sets return the matched value, so valid enum members false and nil are treated as predicate failures by clojure.spec. Generate an explicit contains? predicate for enum specs and add regression tests for boolean false and nil/null enum values.
acceptance_criteria: Enum schemas containing false or nil/null validate those values correctly; existing enum behavior remains unchanged for truthy values; regression tests cover false and nil/null enum members.

title: Add changelog entries for recent user-facing behavior changes
description: CHANGELOG.md was not updated for recent user-facing behavior changes reflected in docs and tests, including array cardinality behavior, minItems/maxItems, enum validation, inline schema handling, and security input handling. Add Unreleased entries so release notes match the body of work.
acceptance_criteria: CHANGELOG.md has an Unreleased section covering the recent user-facing generator behavior changes.
close_reason: CHANGELOG.md now has an Unreleased section covering recent generator behavior changes.

title: Update roadmap for resolved array and inline schema limitations
description: roadmap.md still lists array non-empty defaults and many inline response schemas as current limitations, but the recent work changes array defaults to zero-or-more and adds inline/nested  validation coverage. Move resolved items into supported behavior or narrow the limitations to remaining unsupported cases.
acceptance_criteria: roadmap.md accurately distinguishes supported array cardinality and inline/nested response schema behavior from remaining limitations.
close_reason: Updated roadmap to distinguish supported array cardinality and inline/nested response schema validation from remaining limitations.

title: Align README parameter forwarding note with implementation
description: README says only parameters declared in the OpenAPI spec are forwarded, but generate-test-form currently forwards every non-path :params entry as query params and every :headers entry without checking operation parameter declarations. Either update the implementation to filter by declared parameters or document the actual passthrough behavior.
acceptance_criteria: README and implementation agree on parameter/header forwarding behavior; tests cover the chosen behavior if implementation changes.
close_reason: README now documents the existing pass-through behavior for params and headers.

title: Report generated-test harness exceptions as failures
description: The new generated-test e2e helpers catch and discard Throwable. A generated test that throws before reporting can produce zero failures/errors and falsely pass. Update the harness to run through clojure.test/test-var or report caught exceptions with t/report so counters reflect failures.
acceptance_criteria: Generated test helper exceptions increment error/failure counters; positive tests fail if the generated test throws before assertions; affected e2e tests still pass for valid generated output.
close_reason: Generated-test harness exceptions now report clojure.test errors and regression coverage verifies counters increment.

title: Handle OpenAPI security OR alternatives
description: OpenAPI security arrays are OR alternatives, but generated request code currently selects only the first requirement object. If credentials are supplied for a later valid alternative, generated requests omit them and fail unnecessarily. Support selecting a satisfiable alternative from the provided security input, or explicitly document and test a deliberate first-alternative-only limitation.
acceptance_criteria: Generated requests can use credentials for a non-first valid security alternative, or the limitation is explicit in docs and tests; regression tests cover an operation with apiKey OR bearer alternatives and credentials for the second alternative.

title: Use canonical Beads upstream URL consistently
description: .beads/README.md links to github.com/steveyegge/beads while project instructions point to github.com/gastownhall/beads. Use one canonical upstream URL throughout the repository to avoid confusing future operators and agents.
acceptance_criteria: Beads-related documentation in the repository uses the same canonical upstream URL consistently.

title: Clean up duplicated and placeholder agent instructions
description: Agent-facing instructions contain duplicated Beads guidance across AGENTS.md and generated sections, and CLAUDE.md still includes placeholder build/test, architecture, and convention sections. Consolidate the guidance so future agents see one authoritative Beads/Codex instruction path plus the local non-interactive shell guidance and real project notes.
acceptance_criteria: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md no longer contain empty placeholder sections or conflicting duplicated Beads instructions; Codex and Claude still point to ai/AGENT_RULES.md and Beads workflow guidance.

title: Update deftestgen docstring for security inputs
description: The public deftestgen macro docstring says each input has two optional keys and omits :security, while README documents :params, :headers, and :security. Update the docstring to describe :security and supported scheme behavior consistently with implementation and README.
acceptance_criteria: deftestgen docstring documents :security alongside :params and :headers, including supported scheme behavior or limitations.

title: Avoid unresolved macro-generated vars in e2e tests
description: The new e2e tests reference macro-generated vars with reader var syntax such as #'test-get-..., which clj-kondo can report as unresolved. Use the resolved vars returned by generated-tests or resolve dynamically with ns-resolve to avoid lint noise and CI failures.
acceptance_criteria: E2E tests no longer reference macro-generated test vars with unresolved reader syntax; tests still execute the intended generated vars; linting should not report unresolved generated test vars.

title: Expand security scheme test coverage
description: README documents bearer, http basic, and header apiKey security support, but current tests primarily exercise bearer plus one header apiKey example. Add focused coverage for apply-security or generated request execution covering basic auth, missing credentials, unsupported/non-header apiKey schemes, and top-level security fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover basic auth, missing credentials, unsupported or non-header apiKey handling, and top-level security fallback without weakening existing security behavior.
